name: Laughlin AFB
tagline: The Air Force's busiest pilot-training base, on the Texas-Mexico border at Del Rio.
_note: Air Force pilot-training base (Del Rio, TX, on the border; Central time). CONUS full template (BAH MHA TX278 Laughlin AFB/Del Rio; RentCast/SchoolDigger work). 47th Flying Training Wing; one of the AF's busiest Undergraduate Pilot Training (UPT) bases. Remote small border town. Medical: 47th Medical Group clinic. Facts verified from official sources (laughlin.af.mil, 47 MDG/TRICARE, DeCA/AAFES, Wikipedia for corroborated headline facts). Texas moved to TRICARE West in 2024; no state income tax. Medium-confidence flags: base coords (approx), off-base ZIP 78840 (Del Rio).

data.Mission: Undergraduate Pilot Training (UPT); one of the AF's busiest pilot-training bases
data.Setting: Small, remote border town (affordable, no state income tax)
data.Note: Lake Amistad is right next door

sections.title: Mission & Identity
data: Laughlin Air Force Base is the home of the 47th Flying Training Wing and one of the Air Force's busiest pilot-training bases, where new aviators go through Undergraduate Pilot Training. The flight line runs hard year-round.
It's at Del Rio on the Texas-Mexico border, a small, remote, affordable town next to Lake Amistad, far from a big city but with a tight base community.

sections.title: A Small, Remote Border Town: Lean Into the Community

data.subtitle: Del Rio is small and remote, so the housing choice is simple (on base or in town), and the bigger adjustment is the isolation, which the base community helps offset.
intro: Del Rio is affordable but far from a major city, so most families live on base or in town. The cost of living is low and there's no state income tax.
The real adjustment is the remoteness; the base community is close-knit, and Lake Amistad and the outdoors are the local draw.

recommendations.advice: On-base housing keeps the commute short and the community close, a popular choice given the remote location.
Del Rio offers affordable housing a short drive from the gate.
Lake Amistad (boating, fishing) is the local highlight; lean into the outdoors.
San Antonio is about 2.5 hours east for big-city needs; plan trips and stock up.

sections.title: Gates & Access
data: Visitors without a DoD ID need a visitor pass and a REAL ID-compliant ID from the Visitor Control Center.
Gate hours and visitor requirements change, so confirm the current details on the official Laughlin AFB site before you go.

sections.title: On-Base Housing
data: Laughlin AFB has privatized on-base family housing, popular given the small, remote town.
Eligibility and availability vary by rank and family size, so start with the housing office for the current manager, options, and waitlists.

source.label: DoD 2026 BAH Rates, MHA TX278 (Laughlin AFB/Del Rio)

sections.title: Schools
data: There is no DoDEA district here; children attend the local civilian public schools. San Felipe Del Rio CISD serves Del Rio and the base.
Your assigned schools depend on your address, so confirm the campus for any neighborhood you are considering.

data.Care: Outpatient primary care on base; inpatient, emergency, and specialty care via local providers and (for complex needs) San Antonio

intro: Whether to rent or buy is the biggest money decision of most PCS moves. At small, remote Laughlin many families take on-base housing, and pilot-training tours are often short.
rent: Your tour is a UPT pipeline or your orders feel uncertain.
You prefer on-base housing for the short commute and community.
You'd rather not own in a small, isolated market.
Your BAH comfortably covers rent if you go off base.
buy: You expect a longer instructor tour and want to settle in.
You want to use your VA loan and build equity instead of paying rent.
The very affordable Del Rio market fits your budget for a home you like.
You're comfortable with a small resale market.
data.bottom_line: There is no universal right answer. Del Rio is inexpensive, so BAH stretches, but the small, isolated market affects resale, so many take on-base housing. Run your BAH against the rent and home prices above, and talk to a lender and an agent who work with military families before you commit.

intro: The first month at a new duty station is mostly logistics, and at a remote base, planning ahead helps.

steps.title: Line up temporary lodging
steps.text: Book on-base lodging or a short-term rental before you arrive. You can claim Temporary Lodging Expense (TLE) for a limited number of days while you house-hunt.

steps.title: In-process and update DEERS
steps.text: Report to your unit and finance, and make sure DEERS and ID cards are current for every family member so TRICARE and base access work.

steps.title: Start with the Housing Office
steps.text: On-base housing is popular here; get on the list early and get the office's read on Del Rio.

steps.title: Register your vehicle and sort access
steps.text: Handle base access and Texas vehicle registration, and learn the visitor-pass process for guests. Bring your registration and insurance.

steps.title: Enroll the kids in school
steps.text: Contact San Felipe Del Rio CISD early and bring records and immunizations.

steps.title: Set up healthcare
steps.text: Update your TRICARE enrollment so you can be seen at the 47th Medical Group clinic; San Antonio is the option for complex care.

data.q: What is Laughlin AFB known for?
data.a: It's one of the Air Force's busiest pilot-training bases, home to the 47th Flying Training Wing running Undergraduate Pilot Training.
On-base housing is popular given the remote location; Del Rio also offers affordable housing in town.
Yes. Del Rio is a small border town next to Lake Amistad; San Antonio is about 2.5 hours away for big-city needs.
Laughlin is MHA TX278 (Laughlin AFB/Del Rio). Use the calculator above for your grade.
